text: Race distance (10K, half-marathon, marathon/ultra) is not significantly associated with consumption of most food groups among recreational endurance runners.
10-km runners consume significantly more fruits and vegetables than half-marathon and marathon/ultra-marathon runners.
10-km runners consume significantly more total protein than half-marathon and marathon/ultra-marathon runners.
Older age predicts higher consumption of fruits and vegetables, and plant protein among recreational endurance runners.
Older age predicts lower consumption of unprocessed meat, processed meat, and eggs among recreational endurance runners.

Longer race distance does not appear to drive greater dietary intake diversity or volume in recreational runners, suggesting nutritional guidance should not differ substantially by target race distance alone.
